title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: Twenty-one EU member states, the UK, and six Middle Eastern jurisdictions are simultaneously moving from compliance planning to active enforcement across MiCA licensing, AML supervision, and crypto authorization frameworks. The EU's Anti-Money Laundering Authority becomes operational in Q3 with direct supervision authority over highest-risk CASPs, while France's July 1 PSAN-to-MiCA transition deadline and the UK's Q3 authorization application cutoff create urgent infrastructure buildout windows.

regulators.full: European Commission / European Securities and Markets Authority / Anti-Money Laundering Authority
regulators.focus: MiCA implementation, AMLA launch, AML package, DORA, RTS adoption
